mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.50% and remains there for at least one month, equity risk premiums compress as the cost of capital declines. This monetary easing signal produces positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as investors rebalance into equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.11% indicates deep easing regime
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with near-zero rates
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.50%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 100,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion. This expansion supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon as the market reprices growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns show zero or negative correlation with month-over-month PAYEMS growth, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in both retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) over a 3-month period signals weakening aggregate demand. This demand contraction reduces earnings expectations and increases equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 5% over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 441,363 million dollars, HOUST at 897 thousand units
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal shows lower predictive power than either series alone (Rank IC difference > 0.05 in favor of single series).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 1% month-over-month indicates accelerating supply-side capacity utilization. This supply expansion supports profit margins and reduces inflation pressures, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon as the market reprices stagflation risk downward.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following INDPRO > 1% MoM growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises more than 0.5% month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, a real rate compression occurs. This negative real rate regime incentivizes equity allocation as a hedge against currency debasement, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in the negative real rate regime do not exceed baseline returns by at least 200 basis points on average, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises more than 0.3 percentage points in a single month, it signals an unexpected labor market deterioration. This shock triggers a flight-to-safety response, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as investors reduce equity exposure.
regime_hint: labor market shock or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances following UNRATE spikes > 0.3pp, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter above 2.0% annualized signals sustained economic expansion. This expansion supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as the market reprices growth duration upward.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 16,294.7 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns show zero or negative correlation with quarterly GDPC1 growth rates, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) declines more than 1.5% over a 3-month period while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated, a stagflation signal emerges. This stagflation regime compresses equity valuations as investors face simultaneous growth and inflation headwinds, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1.5% over 3 months AND CPIAUCSL MoM > 0.3%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of stagflation-triggered instances, or the joint signal shows lower predictive power than INDPRO decline alone (Rank IC difference > 0.04 in favor of single series).
